name: Leonese

ancestors.notes: Common Asturleonese trunk. Leonese diverged southward during the Reconquista, developing its own written tradition in the Kingdom of León.
Celtic substrate of the Asturian-Leonese highlands, shared with central Asturian.
Suebi superstrate, somewhat lighter in León than in the Asturian coast.
Visigothic superstrate: the Leonese meseta was more thoroughly Visigothic than coastal Asturias.
Andalusi Arabic: more intense in León than in Asturias, given the longer Moorish occupation of the meseta and the Reconquista repopulation from south.
Mozarabic adstrate: significant in the repopulation of the Leonese meseta, as Mozarabic-speaking Christians from al-Andalus joined the Reconquista settlements.

notes: Medieval and modern Leonese (llingua llionesa / leonés). KEY GRAPHEME→PHONEME CORRESPONDENCES grounded in Morala & Egido (2009) and the Propuesta de Norma Ortográfica (anonymous, undated): (1) ⟨x⟩ = /ʃ/ (dorsopalatal fricative sorda, unanimous across all text models — Source B §2.3; Source A pp. 10, 15). WARNING: ⟨x⟩ applies ONLY where Castilian has ⟨j/ge/gi⟩ (velar origin). When Castilian has ⟨y/ll⟩ (palatal -LJ- etc.) Leonese uses ⟨y⟩, NOT ⟨x⟩ — *meyorar* not *mexorar*, *espeyu* not *espexar* (Source B §2.3; Source A p. 15). (2) ⟨ll⟩ = /ʎ/ (palatal lateral) from Latin L- initial and -LL-: *llugar, lluz, llobu* — mandatory for patrimonial vocabulary (Source B §2.2; Source A p. 10). Not applicable to PL-/CL-/FL- which give ⟨ch-⟩. (3) ⟨ch⟩ = /tʃ/ (africada palatal) from PL-, CL-, FL-: *chamar, chover, chegar* (Source B §3.5; Source A p. 16). (4) ⟨y⟩ = /ʝ/ (palatal sonora) from -LJ-, -DJ-, -GJ-, -C'L-: *meyor, fiyu, peleya* (Source B §1, §3.4; Source A p. 10). Alto Sil/Laciana admit /tʃ/ variant for -LJ-: *trabachar, mechor*. (5) ⟨ñ⟩ = /ɲ/ — general Leonese baseline (Source B §1); -NN- > /n/ simplification legitimate in NW zone: *cabana, pena*. NOTE: che vaqueira (ts/l.l) is Laciana/Alto Sil only (see ast-x-occidental). 4-WAY SIBILANT CONTRAST preserved in archaic/medieval register. Sources: Morala & Egido (2009); Propuesta de Norma Ortográfica.

sources.author: Morala Rodríguez, J.R. & Egido Fernández, M.C.
sources.title: A vueltas con una norma para el leonés
sources.publisher: Instituto Castellano y Leonés de la Lengua, Colección Beltenebros 23, in El Leonés en el siglo XXI
sources.notes: ISBN: 978-84-936383-8-2. Descriptive/analytical survey of Leonese written texts; not prescriptive. Confirms grapheme values from corpora.

sources.author: Anonymous
sources.title: Propuesta de Norma Ortográfica para el Leonés: Hacia una codificación coherente, patrimonial y dialectalmente inclusiva
sources.notes: Prescriptive orthographic proposal; explicitly cites Morala/Egido 2009. Western Leonese base. Year is approximate (undated document).

sources.author: García Arias, X.Ll.
sources.title: Gramática histórica de la lengua asturiana

location.notes: the province of León — the core of the Leonese area. [Wikidata Q71140 P625]
